ADDITIONAL_INFORMATION: This report prepared by Wade Sharpe with the Gas Authority of Georgia with information submitted by Terry Conyer Gas Superintendent for the City of Hawkinsville Georgia.

We have replaced all of our commercial and industrial meters and correctors to ensure as accurate of a reading as possible. We replaced our check meter (take-off meter) in Carlisle and installed a new corrector on it as well to ensure it is as accurate and as close to our gas company's readings as we can possibly get. We have changed WHEN we bill out so we are billing off of our actual gas BILL rather than off of an estimate and then having to true-up every month. Our utility workers read as close to the last day of the ending month and the first day of the upcoming month to match how our gas company bills us (they bill us from the first day of the month to the last day of the month). Our utility workers are diligent to be looking for any signs of leaks every month when they are out reading meters. We now know when we repair a leak and gas is lost to run a calculation to figure out about how much gas was lost during the repair. By doing all these things we have been able to get our unaccounted gas at a consistent percentage but we are still working to get it down even more if we possibly can.

Unaccounted Gas: The City of Trinidad believes the high percentage for unaccounted gas appears to be an ongoing issue that we are working to resolve. The reason that we believe that it is occurring is partly due to how our billing process utilizes four billing cycles, which results in our gas meters being read in a manner that captures parts of the previous month into the current month. We strongly believe that this practice may be contributing to a skewed percentage of unaccounted gas losses, and we are confident that the integrity of our system is intact and that there are no leaks. We are proposing to our billing department to change when the meters are read so that each meters are being read based on full calendar months. This adjustment could enable us to better track our gas consumption and pinpoint any discrepancies more accurately.
This annual report was prepared by Maurice Chaney, Regional Manager of Subscribe Regulatory Compliance Service, using information provided by Mike Hughes, Gas Superintendent for the Town of Trion.
